title: Caspofungin compositions for inhalation
abstract: Compositions and corresponding methods for the treatment and/or prevention of a fungal infection in the pulmonary system of a subject in need thereof with caspofungin or a derivative thereof are disclosed herein.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A composition suitable for inhalation administration comprising i) caspofungin, or a polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof; and ii) polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP). 2. The composition of claim 1, wherein the pharmaceutically acceptable salt of caspofungin is the acetate salt. 3. The composition of claim 2, wherein the composition comprises from about 1 mg to about 100 mg of caspofungin acetate. 4. The composition of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the weight ratio of polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) to caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, is from about 6: 1 to about 1 : 1. 5. The composition of claim 4, wherein the weight ratio of polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP) to caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof is about 4: 1. 6. The composition of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the composition further comprises at least one stability-enhancing salt. 7. The composition of claim 6, wherein the at least one stability-enhancing salt is selected from the group consisting of sodium chloride, potassium chloride, sodium phosphate, potassium phosphate, or a combination thereof. 8. The composition of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the composition further comprises a pH modifier. 9. The composition of claim 8, wherein the pH modifier is sodium hydroxide or acetic acid. 10. The composition of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the composition has a pH of from about 5 to about 7. 11. The composition of any one of claims 1-10, wherein the composition further comprises a vehicle. 12. The composition of claim 11, wherein the vehicle is selected from the group consisting of water, saline, or phosphate-buffered saline. 13. The composition of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the composition is a pre-lyophilized composition. 14. The composition of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the composition is a lyophilized composition. 15. The composition of claim 14, wherein the composition is a lyophilized composition that has been reconstituted with water, saline, or phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) to provide a reconstituted solution. 16. The composition of claim 15, wherein the reconstituted solution comprises greater than about 95% of caspofungin, or a polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof that has not degraded after storage at about 5 Â°C for at least 240 hours or has not degraded after storage at about 25 Â°C for 7 hours. 17. The composition of any one of claims 1-16, wherein administration of the composition using an inhalation delivery device provides a lung tissue concentration of caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, that is from about 25-fold to about lOO-fold greater than intravenous administration at the same delivery dose. 18. The composition of claim 17, wherein the lung tissue concentration of caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, administered using the inhalation delivery device is greater than intravenous administration at the same delivery dose for about 0.5 hour to about 168 hours. 19. The composition of any one of claims 1-18, wherein administration of the composition provides a concentration of caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, in the lung that is from about 5-fold greater to about 1000-fold greater than a concentration of caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, in the liver, kidney, or plasma. 20. The composition of claim 19, wherein the concentration of caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, in the lung is greater than a concentration of caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, in the liver, kidney, or plasma for about 0.5 hour to about 168 hours. 21. The composition of any one of claims 1-20, wherein the concentration of caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, in the lung is above the minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) or the minimum effective concentration (MEC) for about 3 hours after administration. 22. The composition of claim 21, wherein the concentration of caspofungin, or the polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, in the lung is above the minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) or the minimum effective concentration (MEC) for about 168 hours after administration. 23. The composition of claim 21 or 22, wherein the minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) or the minimum effective concentration (MEC) is from about 0.001 pg/mL to about 32 pg/mL. 24. The composition of any one of claims 1-23, wherein the composition is formulated for administration with an inhalation device selected from a jet nebulizer, ultrasonic wave nebulizer, high efficiency nebulizer, heat vaporizer, soft mist inhaler, thermal aerosol inhaler, or electrohydrodynamic-based solution misting inhaler. 25. The composition of any one of claims 1-24, wherein the half-life of the caspofungin, or a polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, in the lung is about 24 hours to about 50 hours. 26. The composition of any one of claims 1-25, wherein the composition provides an increase of greater than about 50% of caspofungin, or a polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof, deposit in the lung without increasing systemic exposure when compared to a composition that does not comprise polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP). 27. The composition of any one of claims 1-26, wherein the composition comprises greater than about 95% of caspofungin, or a polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof that has not degraded after storage at about -20 Â°C, 5 Â°C, or 25 Â°C for about 0 weeks, about 2 weeks, about 4 weeks, about 8 weeks, about 12 weeks or about 52 weeks. 28. The composition of any one of claims 1-5, wherein the composition consists essentially of (i) caspofungin, or a polymorph, pharmaceutically acceptable salt, hydrate, or solvate thereof; and (ii) polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP). 29. A method of preventing or treating a fungal infection in the pulmonary system of a subject in need thereof comprising administering to the subject a composition of any one of claims 1-28. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the method is for treating or preventing a fungal infection caused by Candida sp. , Aspergillus sp., and/or Pneumocystis jirovecii.
